title: cinnamomum sumatranum
intro:
Cinnamomum sumatranum, also known as Sumatran cinnamon, is a species of evergreen tree native to Indonesia, belonging to the family Lauraceae and closely related to the more commonly known Ceylon cinnamon (C. verum).

Medicinally, the bark of C. sumatranum contains oils with antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. Its health benefits include reducing inflammation and improving digestion, as well as exhibiting potential anti-diabetic and anti-cancer properties. The bark can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
